mnemonics.title: DFA Components - 5Q'SF
mnemonics.content: Remember DFA = (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F) using 'Queens Σdelta q0 For' - Queen (Q) runs the show, Σ (Sigma) provides input, δ (delta) drives transitions, q0 is the starting queen, F are the finishing queens.

mnemonics.title: DFA Acceptance Rule
mnemonics.content: DFA accepts if 'Start to Finish' - Start at q0, read entire string, and END in a state from F (Final states). Simple as S.E.F (Start-End-Final).

mnemonics.title: Transition Function Mapping
mnemonics.content: δ: Q × Σ → Q means 'State times Symbol goes to State' - Think of it as: Current Location (Q) + What you read (Σ) = Next Location (Q)

acronyms.usage: A finite state machine where every state has exactly one transition for each input symbol
Formal definition: States, Alphabet, Transition function, Initial state, Final states
String of length zero; in DFA, no transition on ε is allowed in standard definition

visualTips.visualization: Draw circles for states, arrows with input labels for transitions, double-circle final states, and an arrow pointing to initial state from nowhere
Rows = states, Columns = input symbols, Cell (q,s) = δ(q,s). Easy to verify completeness and trace computations.
Follow the path: start at q0, for each input symbol follow the arrow to next state, check if final state reached. Like following a treasure map!

analogies.analogy: Like a security scanner that checks each item (input symbol) and moves to different modes (states). It accepts only if it ends in 'clear' mode (final state) after scanning everything.
Just as a vending machine (DFA) takes coins (input), updates its state (total amount), and only dispenses product (accepts) when it reaches the required amount (final state).
